content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature: add custom filters support to prompt decorator (filters param, create_jinja_env helper). Touches outlines/prompts.py. What does your feature touch?

fields.note: My feature: add custom filters support to prompt decorator (filters param, create_jinja_env helper). Touches outlines/prompts.py. What does your feature touch?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Both our features only touch outlines/prompts.py, so a disjoint file split isn't possible here. Let's accept the overlap and coordinate the design instead, then merge via git after. My plan: add optional 'filters' dict param to @prompt decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) usage), thread filters through Prompt.from_str/from_file, and add a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that centralizes Environment creation + registers the predefined filters (name/description/source) plus any custom ones. Can you share your Global Filter Registry design (function/API names, e.g. register_filter/get_registry) so I can make create_jinja_env consult your registry too, minimizing merge conflicts?

fields.note: Both our features only touch outlines/prompts.py, so a disjoint file split isn't possible here. Let's accept the overlap and coordinate the design instead, then merge via git after. My plan: add optional 'filters' dict param to @prompt decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) usage), thread filters through Prompt.from_str/from_file, and add a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that centralizes Environment creation + registers the predefined filters (name/description/source) plus any custom ones. Can you share your Global Filter Registry design (function/API names, e.g. register_filter/get_registry) so I can make create_jinja_env consult your registry too, minimizing merge conflicts?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed: I implement first, send DONE, you merge my branch and layer the global registry on top. Signatures: prompt(fn=None, *, filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None) - supports bare @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); Prompt.from_str(cls, content: str, filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None); Prompt.from_file(cls, path, filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None); create_jinja_env(filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None) -> Environment - builds the Environment, registers built-in predefined filters (name, description, source), then updates env.filters with the passed-in custom filters dict (custom filters override predefined ones on key collision). I'll send DONE once implemented and pushed.

fields.note: Agreed: I implement first, send DONE, you merge my branch and layer the global registry on top. Signatures: prompt(fn=None, *, filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None) - supports bare @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); Prompt.from_str(cls, content: str, filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None); Prompt.from_file(cls, path, filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None); create_jinja_env(filters: dict[str, Callable] | None = None) -> Environment - builds the Environment, registers built-in predefined filters (name, description, source), then updates env.filters with the passed-in custom filters dict (custom filters override predefined ones on key collision). I'll send DONE once implemented and pushed.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented and pushed to team/agent1 (commit 3111f3ae). Summary: prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(path, filters=None); new create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) centralizes Environment creation, registers predefined filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) then does env.filters.update(filters) so custom filters override on collision. _template_from_str/_template_from_file now just call create_jinja_env(...). All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass. Feel free to fetch/merge team/agent1 and layer your global registry on top.

All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass. Feel free to fetch/merge team/agent1 and layer your global registry on top.
